expectations.evidence: Explicitly states: "Without the atlassian-rest skill, there is no structured workflow (e.g., capture-tasks-from-meeting-notes) to guide action item extraction."

expectations.text: Extracts at least 4 action items from the notes
expectations.evidence: Extracted 5 action items in the table: Sarah (payment integration), Mike (CI pipeline tests), Lisa (API docs), Unassigned (deployment runbook), John (memory leak).

expectations.text: Identifies Sarah, Mike, and Lisa as assignees
expectations.evidence: All three identified as assignees. User lookups attempted: Sarah found as Sarah (Minjung) Kang, Mike found 2 matches, Lisa found as lisa.xu.

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs lookup-user for each unique assignee
expectations.evidence: Uses MCP plugin lookupJiraAccountId, not jira.mjs lookup-user. States: "Without jira.mjs or similar helper scripts, there is no streamlined way."

expectations.text: Presents a table or list of extracted tasks for user confirmation before creating
expectations.evidence: Lists proposed tickets but notes the task "cannot be completed" due to wrong instance. No actual confirmation step was performed -- just a proposed ticket breakdown.

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs create for each confirmed task with --parent or appropriate flags
expectations.evidence: Proposes using createJiraIssue MCP tool, not jira.mjs create. Task could not be completed due to wrong Atlassian instance access.
Does not reference these files. Mentions in "What the Atlassian Skill Would Provide" section that the skill would provide these references, confirming they were not used.
